explanation: This property encodes that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), eventually (within the same step, as this is not a temporal delay) the message is forwarded to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and until the message is either forwarded or the property is vacuously true (e.g., the premise never happens), it is not registered by the Duplicate Set (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set) until after forwarding. This captures the requirement that Local Node must forward the TC message to the Duplicate Set, after which, if not already registered, Duplicate Set registers the message. The empty alternative in the operator is reflected by the structure of the LTL formula, which ensures registration is not performed until forwarding occurs or never at all (if forwarding never happens).

The property states that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it must eventually be followed by a forwarding to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and, if the message has not been registered yet, by a registration in the Duplicate Set (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). The reference to the 'alternative operator with an empty alternative' suggests using the 'until' operator (U) to express the requirement that forwarding continues until registration, or that if not registered, the registration process will always follow a forwarding. This is a typical cause-effect chain, and template 85 captures the direct sequencing: (A) U[0,] (B).

The property requires that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), eventually the message is forwarded to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and, if the message was not yet registered, eventually the Duplicate Set will perform a registration (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). The chosen template expresses that after tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node, we must see msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set, unless msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set cannot occur; and after msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set, unless it cannot occur, we eventually see registration_performed_by_duplicate_set (if the message was not yet registered). This most closely follows the described sequential requirement and is expressed as an 'until' chain of actions. It also captures the alternative operator (here, registration may or may not be performed, depending on the state).

The property states that whenever the Local Node forwards a TC message to the Duplicate Set ('msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set'), then at some point in the future a registration must be performed by the Duplicate Set ('registration_performed_by_duplicate_set'). This matches the statement that Duplicate Set must register the message (when not yet registered) after receiving a forwarded TC message, capturing the requirement for every DM_i. We use TRUE for other required boolean expressions by the template, since the template requires all four but we care only about the implication from forwarding to registration.

The property states that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node, eventually the message must be forwarded to the Duplicate Set, and unless/until it is forwarded to the Duplicate Set, it is guaranteed that registration is not performed by the Duplicate Set. Furthermore, as soon as it is forwarded, a registration must eventually be performed by the Duplicate Set (if the message is not yet registered).

This matches the requirement that a TC message being received at the Local Node must lead to two actions in sequence: first, forwarding to the Duplicate Set, then registration by the Duplicate Set (if needed), with no alternative behavior, as described, and for any DM_i. The LTL fragment F(tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node) -> (F[0,0]((!registration_performed_by_duplicate_set) U[0,] (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set)) | G[0,] !registration_performed_by_duplicate_set) follows the typical pattern of requiring that after an event, a certain action happens before another, unless that second action never happens.

This property formalizes that whenever a TC message is sent from a DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it is always the case that eventually (i.e., in the future) the message is forwarded to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and if that forwarding happens and the message is not yet registered, the Duplicate Set then performs a registration (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). In LTL, this causality is best captured by stating that globally, whenever tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node happens, it must be followed (within any time, so unbounded) by a forwarding to the Duplicate Set, after which, if it was not already registered, registration is performed. Template 93 allows for the specification of such behavior, capturing the implication that the prerequisite (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node) leads to the forwarding event, which in turn must be followed by the registration. Here, we focus only on the described sequence as it is required for every DM_i.

The property states that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it is eventually followed by the message being forwarded to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set). Furthermore, if the message is not yet registered, this is followed by a registration performed by the Duplicate Set (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). The LTL formula uses the until operator to express the sequential forwarding and registration after the TC message is sent. This captures the described requirement that a TC message triggering from DM_i to Local Node must result in a forwarding to Duplicate Set, and if registration is needed, that should also occur. Chosen template 85 fits the sequential relationship of the events.

This property ensures that any time a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (atomic proposition: tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it must be eventually forwarded to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and if the message is not yet registered, the Duplicate Set will perform the registration (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). The clause specifies that forwarding to Duplicate Set occurs and, if registration is required, it is performed. The empty alternative in the original description maps to allowing the property to terminate if the message was already registered (i.e., if registration is not performed, nothing bad happens—thus no strict requirement for registration if already present). The chosen template 85 '((bool_exp3) U[0,] (bool_exp4))' naturally captures the forwarding followed by the registration (if required). N_DURATION1 is set arbitrarily to 5 (can be any integer > 0 for timing, but here the property is untimed). The property is meant to hold for every DM_i, so quantification is left implicit and should be handled in system modeling.

This property expresses that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), it is always followed by the forwarding of the message to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and if the message is not yet registered, a registration is performed by the Duplicate Set (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). The use of the 'alternative operator with an empty alternative' is analogous to the weak-until (W) operator, which means registration must happen if the message hasn't already been registered; otherwise, forwarding to Duplicate Set alone is sufficient. Thus, we encode: Whenever 'tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node' occurs, always, if 'msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set' happens, then weakly until a 'registration_performed_by_duplicate_set' occurs (covering the registration in the not-yet-registered case), otherwise 'msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set' alone suffices. This applies for every DM_i, as the property is general.

This property formalizes that whenever a TC message is sent from DM_i to the Local Node (tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node), then it is always the case that, eventually, the message will be forwarded to the Duplicate Set (msg_forwarded_to_duplicate_set), and—if the message is not yet registered—a registration will be performed by the Duplicate Set (registration_performed_by_duplicate_set). The empty alternative in natural language corresponds to 'unless already registered,' so the registration is only required if it's not registered. Thus, we use an LTL pattern expressing that if the TC message arrives, then (globally) it must eventually be forwarded to the Duplicate Set, and, if needed, registration is performed. This can be structured as: 'whenever a TC message is sent to the Local Node, then globally, if not yet registered, a registration must eventually occur (after being forwarded to Duplicate Set)'. Since we don't have an explicit 'not yet registered' flag, we capture only the required event sequence. The LTL formula uses implication to model this requirement for every DM_i (globally on all executions).
